steps.title: Separate the explanation
steps.content: The first sentence can end after works. The next idea begins with a because-clause explaining why the small hole produces a clear image, keeping the cause attached to the effect.

steps.title: Avoid a splice or run-on
steps.content: Choice A fuses the ideas awkwardly. Choice C creates a comma splice, and Choice D adds it's because before another clause, leaving the sentence tangled. A period after works cleanly starts the explanatory sentence. The answer is B.
